test_description: This test case evaluates the generator model's accuracy and technical depth, assuming a perfect retrieval process.
question: [Fill] In the context of Through Glass Vias (TGVs) used in electronic packaging, the dielectric liner applied on the internal wall of a TGV must have a viscosity less than _____ Poise to ensure proper formation of a dielectric layer with tensile strength greater than about 8 MPa and low dielectric loss.

gold_context_sources.evidence: The method may also include forming a coating of a curable polymer material on an internal wall of a TGV of the plurality of TGVs. A viscosity of the curable polymer material may be less than 30 Poise. The method may further include curing the coating of the curable polymer material to form a dielectric liner having a tensile strength greater than about 8 Mpa and a dielectric loss less than about 0.002.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is 30. Reasoning: According to the disclosed method for improving interfacial adhesion of a copper-glass interface in a Through Glass Via (TGV), the curable polymer material used as a dielectric liner coating on the internal wall of the TGV must have a viscosity less than 30 Poise. This ensures that the polymer can adequately coat the internal surface to form a dielectric liner with the required tensile strength (>8 MPa) and low dielectric loss (<0.002), critical for the electrical and mechanical reliability of the TGV.
